description: Command delegates to skills/deckling-pptx.md (relative to plugin) which does not exist among scanned artifacts; command cannot function
suggested_fix: Create plugins/deckling/skills/deckling-pptx.md skill file or correct the reference path in the command

description: SKILL.md documents /pm:review as a workflow step but no review.md command file exists; users following the documented gap-filing workflow hit a missing command
suggested_fix: Create plugins/product-management/commands/review.md or remove /pm:review from the documented workflow

description: plugin.json declares version 0.1.0 but plugins/product-management/skills/product-management/SKILL.md declares version: 0.2.0
suggested_fix: Set plugin.json version to 0.2.0 to match SKILL.md

description: Command requires $ARGUMENTS (feature-name) but provides no empty-argument branch; invoked as /pm:prd with no name silently runs gh issue search for empty string
suggested_fix: Add: if $ARGUMENTS is empty, ask user for feature name before proceeding

description: Command requires [challenge-name] argument but provides no handling when invoked with no argument; instructions reference checking if [challenge-name] exists which silently fails on empty
suggested_fix: Add: if no argument given, list available challenges and prompt user to select one

description: Command has no allowed-tools declaration in frontmatter; Claude must infer which tools to use
suggested_fix: Add allowed-tools: [Read, Glob] to frontmatter

description: Command has no allowed-tools declaration in frontmatter
suggested_fix: Add allowed-tools: [Read, Write] to frontmatter

description: Command has no allowed-tools declaration in frontmatter
suggested_fix: Add allowed-tools: [Read] to frontmatter

description: Command has no allowed-tools declaration in frontmatter
suggested_fix: Add allowed-tools: [Read] to frontmatter

description: Command has no allowed-tools declaration in frontmatter
suggested_fix: Add allowed-tools: [Read, Write] to frontmatter

description: Command has no allowed-tools declaration in frontmatter
suggested_fix: Add allowed-tools: [Read, Write] to frontmatter

description: Command has no allowed-tools declaration; needs at minimum Bash to run the worker script
suggested_fix: Add allowed-tools: [Bash] to frontmatter

description: Command has no output format section; user cannot anticipate what file will be produced or where it will be saved
suggested_fix: Add Output section: describes generated .pptx filename convention and save location

description: Multi-step Instructions section gives two sequential steps as unnumbered prose without ordering markers
suggested_fix: Number the instructions: 1. Use the deckling-pptx skill... 2. Run the worker script at...

description: Command accepts a topic argument but provides no fallback when invoked with no argument
suggested_fix: Add: If no topic provided, ask the user what presentation they want before running the script

description: $ARGUMENTS used as required feature-name with no empty guard; see Bug #4
suggested_fix: Add empty-argument branch that prompts for feature name

description: [challenge-name] argument required but no handling when omitted; see Bug #5
suggested_fix: List available challenges and prompt for selection when no argument given

description: 'appropriate' used without concrete spec in 'Trials configured with appropriate period'
suggested_fix: Replace with concrete guidance, e.g. '7-14 day trial period'

description: 'appropriately sized' used before the concrete spec (44x44px); the qualifier is redundant given the immediate measurement
fp_reason: The same sentence immediately provides the concrete minimum (44x44px), making 'appropriately sized' a stylistic lead-in rather than an undefined vague claim
rule_gap: Rule R09 should allow vague qualifier immediately followed by a concrete measurement on the same line

description: 'relevant' used in quality standards: 'Focus on information relevant to competitive positioning'
suggested_fix: Replace with: 'Focus on pricing, features, hiring signals, and review sentiment'

description: Outbound HTTPS call to Anthropic API; uses ANTHROPIC_API_KEY from environment; key never logged or forwarded elsewhere
suggested_fix: No action required; standard and documented usage

description: Outbound HTTPS to Telegram Bot API using token read from plaintext .streak/config.md; token could be accidentally committed
suggested_fix: Read TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN from environment variable instead of markdown config file

description: subprocess.run for git pull/push; commit message includes user-supplied challenge name from Telegram; safe because list invocation is used (no shell=True), git receives name as literal string argument
fp_reason: subprocess.run with a list and no shell=True cannot be exploited via shell metacharacter injection regardless of string content
rule_gap: SEC-shell-true should distinguish list-form subprocess.run (safe) from string-form or shell=True invocations

description: anthropic>=0.39.0 uses >= operator; will automatically upgrade through breaking major versions on fresh installs
suggested_fix: Pin to anthropic==0.39.0 (or latest tested version); update explicitly when bumping

description: pytz>=2024.1 uses >= operator; unpinned minor/major versions reduce reproducibility
suggested_fix: Pin to pytz==2024.1

description: SKILL.md top-level formula shows WINNING = Pain x Timing x Execution Capability (3 factors, multiplicative) but gap-analyst.md and gaps.md implement 6 additive factors scored 0-60; the tagline formula is misleading
suggested_fix: Update SKILL.md formula line to match implementation: WINNING = Pain + Timing + Execution + Fit + Revenue + Moat (max 60)

description: /pm:review documented as a workflow step but no review.md command exists; same as Bug #2
suggested_fix: Create plugins/product-management/commands/review.md implementing the gap review flow

description: skills/deckling-pptx.md referenced as skill entry point does not exist; same as Bug #1
suggested_fix: Create the missing skill file to bridge command and worker script

description: plugin.json version 0.1.0 vs SKILL.md version 0.2.0; same as Bug #3
suggested_fix: Bump plugin.json to 0.2.0
